core_concept: Animals: Living Things with Bodies, Needs, and Homes
facts: Animals are living things, so they need food and water to stay alive; a rock or toy is not living and does not need food.
A fish is an animal that lives in water habitats like lakes or oceans, while a bird is an animal that lives on land in forests or farms.
Every animal has a body and can move: a bird moves by flying, a snake moves by slithering, and a fish moves by swimming.
misconceptions: Some children think that all animals live on land and have legs, so they do not consider fish or snakes to be animals.
